root.title: Turn 1: The Casual Hook
root.text: Let's figure out what's going on with you today. Pick the word that hits closest to your energy right now.

node_t2_overwhelmed.title: Turn 2: Overwhelmed Validation
node_t2_overwhelmed.text: That's a lot to deal with. Usually, when things feel that heavy, it's because you're trying to manage a bunch of expectations that shouldn't even be yours to fix.

node_t2_bored.title: Turn 2: Bored Validation
node_t2_bored.text: That's a weirdly frustrating space to be in. People think boredom is peaceful, but it usually means you're completely drained by routines that don't actually matter to you.

node_t2_fine.title: Turn 2: Fine Validation
node_t2_fine.text: Glad to hear it. Honestly, having a stable mind state is great—but sometimes, maintaining that 'fine' status takes a lot of quiet effort to keep background distractions away. You know?

node_t2_pivot_1.title: Turn 2: Pivot Hook
node_t2_pivot_1.text: Fair enough. Human emotions rarely fit into neat little labels anyway. Let's look past the specific words and focus on where your mind goes when it runs free.

node_t3_drift_heavy.title: Turn 3: Heavy Drift Query
node_t3_drift_heavy.text: When you finally get a quiet second alone with that weight, where does your brain usually end up drifting?

node_t3_drift_light.title: Turn 3: Light Drift Query
node_t3_drift_light.text: When you're stuck in those everyday mindless routines, what is your brain actually doing to pass the time?

node_t3_drift_alt.title: Turn 3: Alternate Drift Query
node_t3_drift_alt.text: Thanks for keeping me honest. Let's re-frame it. When you're completely alone with your thoughts, are you mostly trying to map out tomorrow, or parsing through yesterday?

node_t4_future.title: Turn 4: Future Reflection
node_t4_future.text: It sounds like you're constantly trying to solve problems that haven't even happened yet—stressing over an unwritten script. Let's find out where that script is set.

node_t4_past.title: Turn 4: Past Reflection
node_t4_past.text: It's exhausting to look backward. You keep reviewing old footage, playing back choices, and wishing you could rewrite a scene that has already finished rolling.

node_t5_pressure_source.title: Turn 5: Main Pressure Query
node_t5_pressure_source.text: Look at the main stage of your life right now. Where is the absolute loudest background noise coming from?

node_t6_school.title: Turn 6: Academic Path
node_t6_school.text: Yeah, school performance weight. But be honest—is it strictly the grades, or is social stress compounding that academic pressure?

node_t6_social.title: Turn 6: Social Path
node_t6_social.text: Social friction drains so much energy. When friend dynamics get messy, does that anxiety start dragging down your school focus too?

node_t6_pivot_2.title: Turn 6: Second Pivot
node_t6_pivot_2.text: Fair enough. Strict categories fail when things get complicated. Let's track the footprint instead: when stress hits, does it end up souring your schoolwork and your social ties at the same time?

node_t7_both_tracks.title: Turn 7: Both Validation
node_t7_both_tracks.text: That's a brutal crossfire. When you're dealing with academic expectations and social navigation all at once, your mind never gets a safe zone to relax.

node_t7_school_only.title: Turn 7: School Validation
node_t7_school_only.text: So it's purely a performance trap. You're treating your value as a person like a GPA scorecard, working for an internal boss that is never satisfied.

node_t7_social_only.title: Turn 7: Social Validation
node_t7_social_only.text: So the grades are fine, but the connections hurt. It feels like you're constantly auditioning for a role in your own group, wearing a mask just to stay safe.

node_t7_internal_only.title: Turn 7: Internal Validation
node_t7_internal_only.text: Interesting. So the external stuff is stable, but there's an internal restlessness. A quiet friction that stays active even when everything looks fine on paper.

node_t8_behavior_query.title: Turn 8: Behavioral Mechanism
node_t8_behavior_query.text: When that underlying friction builds up, how does it physically manifest in your day-to-day routine?

node_t9_freeze.title: Turn 9: Freeze Response
node_t9_freeze.text: Freezing is an instinctive defense mechanism. If you don't start the task, you can't fail at it. Your brain thinks it's protecting you, but it just keeps you stuck.

node_t9_crash.title: Turn 9: Crash Response
node_t9_crash.text: Running on pure adrenaline only works for a little while. You keep pushing because you're terrified that if you slow down for a second, the whole structure will collapse.

node_t10_escape.title: Turn 10: High-Stress Resting
node_t10_escape.text: And when you try to escape that cycle by scrolling on screens or hiding in your room, it doesn't actually feel restful, does it? It's high-stress resting. Your hands are busy, but your mind is burning fuel.

node_t11_switchboard.title: Turn 11: Final Selection Switchboard
node_t11_switchboard.text: Let's strip away the noise. If you had a button right now to magically clear your plate, what would you want saved first?

branches.text: Just take the academic weight off
Just fix the social connection side

node_t12_end_both.title: Turn 12: End Crossfire Memory
node_t12_end_both.text: We've reached the bottom. It makes sense why you felt running low when we started—fighting a war on two fronts with school performance and social navigation means your survival depends on lowering the stakes. Put down the scripts, close this tab, and take a breath. You don't have to carry it all perfectly today. You're going to be okay.

node_t12_end_school.title: Turn 12: End School Memory
node_t12_end_school.text: We've reached the bottom. Your social world isn't what's draining you; it's the intense weight you've placed on your academic scores. A single test or grade won't break your future, no matter what the background noise says. Put down the study guides, close this tab, and give yourself a night off. You're going to be okay.

node_t12_end_social.title: Turn 12: End Social Memory
node_t12_end_social.text: We've reached the bottom. Your school assignments aren't the issue; it's the sheer exhaustion of trying to navigate changing social ties and worrying about fitting in. Stop forcing yourself into boxes to please characters who don't matter. Close this tab, step away from the notifications, and just be yourself. You're going to be okay.
